sections.heading: Why Test ViewModel and Repository?
sections.content: ViewModel and Repository are the two most critical layers in Android's MVVM architecture — they contain the majority of your app's business logic, data transformation, and state management. Testing them thoroughly in isolation gives you fast, reliable feedback that your core logic works correctly without needing a device, emulator, or real network connection. ViewModel tests verify that UI state is produced correctly in response to user actions and data changes, that navigation events are emitted at the right time, and that errors are handled gracefully. Repository tests verify that the correct data source is called under each condition, that caching logic works correctly, that network errors are propagated or recovered from, and that local and remote data are combined correctly. Together, ViewModel and Repository tests form the backbone of a healthy Android test suite.

rows.what_to_test: UI state transitions, one-shot events, error handling, argument parsing from SavedStateHandle
Caching strategy, data source selection, error propagation, data mapping

rows.dependencies_to_mock: None — use real in-memory Room database
rows.what_to_test: Query correctness, insert/update/delete behavior, Flow emissions from queries
Business rule application, input validation, data transformation, error mapping
Correct field mapping, null handling, default values, format conversions

sections.heading: Required Dependencies for Architecture Testing
items: JUnit 4: testImplementation("junit:junit:4.13.2")
Mockito Kotlin: testImplementation("org.mockito.kotlin:mockito-kotlin:5.x.x")
Coroutines Test: testImplementation("org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-coroutines-test:1.x.x")
Turbine (Flow testing): testImplementation("app.cash.turbine:turbine:1.x.x")
Google Truth: testImplementation("com.google.truth:truth:1.x.x")
Arch Core Testing (LiveData): testImplementation("androidx.arch.core:core-testing:2.x.x")
Room testing (DAO tests): androidTestImplementation("androidx.room:room-testing:$roomVersion")
Hilt testing: testImplementation("com.google.dagger:hilt-android-testing:$hiltVersion") for unit tests with Hilt

sections.heading: MainDispatcherRule — Essential for ViewModel Tests
sections.content: ViewModels launch coroutines on viewModelScope which uses Dispatchers.Main internally. In unit tests running on the JVM, Dispatchers.Main is not available by default — attempting to use it causes an IllegalStateException with 'Module with Main dispatcher had failed to initialize'. The solution is MainDispatcherRule — a JUnit TestWatcher that replaces Dispatchers.Main with a TestDispatcher before each test and resets it after. This is the single most important setup step for ViewModel unit testing. The rule must be applied to every ViewModel test class using @get:Rule.

sections.heading: Implementing MainDispatcherRule
items: Define the rule once and share it across all test classes: class MainDispatcherRule(val testDispatcher: TestDispatcher = UnconfinedTestDispatcher()) : TestWatcher() { override fun starting(description: Description) { Dispatchers.setMain(testDispatcher) }; override fun finished(description: Description) { Dispatchers.resetMain() } }
Apply in every ViewModel test class: @get:Rule val mainDispatcherRule = MainDispatcherRule()
UnconfinedTestDispatcher — executes coroutines eagerly and immediately without suspending; best for most ViewModel tests where you want state to update synchronously
StandardTestDispatcher — coroutines are queued and only run when explicitly advanced; use with runTest { advanceUntilIdle() } for precise control over execution order
Access the dispatcher in tests: mainDispatcherRule.testDispatcher — pass to coroutine builders that need an explicit dispatcher
Place the rule file in a shared test utility package: com.example.app.test.util.MainDispatcherRule
For tests that need both MainDispatcherRule and InstantTaskExecutorRule, declare both as @get:Rule — order matters if they interact
runTest { } from coroutines-test automatically uses the test dispatcher — combine with MainDispatcherRule for full coverage

rows.approach: Assert the StateFlow's initial value immediately after ViewModel creation — before any action is triggered
Stub repository to return success, trigger load action, advance coroutines, assert success state
Stub repository to throw or return error, trigger load action, advance coroutines, assert error state
Use Turbine to collect all emissions in order and assert each state in sequence
Trigger the user action method on the ViewModel, advance coroutines, assert updated state

sections.heading: Testing StateFlow with Turbine
items: Collect StateFlow emissions in a test block: viewModel.uiState.test { /* assertions */ }
awaitItem() — suspends until the next emission and returns it: val state = awaitItem()
Assert each emission in sequence: assertThat(awaitItem()).isEqualTo(HomeUiState.Loading); assertThat(awaitItem()).isEqualTo(HomeUiState.Success(data))
cancelAndIgnoreRemainingEvents() — cancels collection and ignores any remaining emissions — use at the end of most StateFlow tests
awaitComplete() — asserts the Flow completed normally — use for finite flows
awaitError() — asserts the Flow terminated with an exception: val error = awaitError(); assertThat(error).isInstanceOf(IOException::class.java)
expectNoEvents() — asserts no new emissions have occurred — useful for verifying state did NOT change
ensureAllEventsConsumed() — strict mode — fails if there are unconsumed events; use when you want to assert exact emission count
Test SharedFlow events (navigation, snackbar): viewModel.events.test { onNodeWithTag("button").performClick(); assertThat(awaitItem()).isInstanceOf(NavigateToDetail::class.java) }

rows.use_case: Navigation commands, show snackbar, show dialog — one-shot actions that should not be replayed

rows.test_approach: Use Turbine on the receiveAsFlow(): viewModel.events.test { viewModel.onLoginClicked(); assertThat(awaitItem()).isEqualTo(UiEvent.NavigateToHome) }
rows.use_case: Same as SharedFlow events — Channel guarantees exactly-once delivery even with multiple collectors

rows.test_approach: Use InstantTaskExecutorRule; observe with observeForever; trigger action; assert LiveData value
rows.use_case: Legacy codebases using LiveData for one-shot events

sections.heading: Testing SavedStateHandle in ViewModel
items: SavedStateHandle can be constructed directly in unit tests with initial values: val savedStateHandle = SavedStateHandle(mapOf("userId" to 42))
Pass it to the ViewModel constructor: val viewModel = DetailViewModel(savedStateHandle, mockRepository)
Test that the ViewModel reads arguments correctly: assertThat(viewModel.userId).isEqualTo(42)
Test that the ViewModel loads data for the correct ID: verify(mockRepository).getUser(42)
Test SavedStateHandle state saving: savedStateHandle["key"] = "value"; assertThat(viewModel.someState.value).isEqualTo(expectedState)
Navigation Component passes route arguments via SavedStateHandle — test with the exact key used in the nav graph argument declaration
For nullable arguments: SavedStateHandle(mapOf("userId" to null)) — test null handling in the ViewModel
Test that ViewModel survives process death by verifying state is restored from SavedStateHandle values

sections.heading: Testing the Repository Layer
sections.content: Repository tests verify the coordination logic between data sources — when to fetch from the network, when to serve from cache, how to handle errors, and how to map data between layers. The repository's dependencies (typically a remote API interface and a local DAO) should be mocked so the repository can be tested in complete isolation. For repositories that return Flow, use Turbine to assert emissions. For repositories that implement an offline-first caching strategy, test each scenario separately: cache hit, cache miss with successful network fetch, cache miss with network error, and stale cache refresh.

sections.heading: Repository Test Scenarios
items: Cache hit — local data exists and is fresh: stub DAO to return data; assert repository returns local data without calling the API
Cache miss — local data absent: stub DAO to return empty; stub API to return data; assert API is called and result is saved to DAO and returned
Network error with no cache: stub DAO to return empty; stub API to throw IOException; assert repository emits error state or throws
Network error with stale cache: stub DAO to return stale data; stub API to throw IOException; assert repository returns cached data with error indication
Successful refresh: stub API to return new data; assert new data is saved to DAO via insert/update; assert repository emits updated data
Data mapping: stub API to return a NetworkModel; assert repository correctly maps it to a DomainModel before returning
Concurrent requests: verify the repository does not make duplicate API calls when data is already being fetched
Verify DAO interactions: verify(mockDao).insertAll(expectedEntities) — confirm repository persists data correctly after fetching

rows.description: Returns a finite Flow with the given entities — simulates a DAO query result
Stubs a suspend function to return a value — use in runTest block
Simulates a network failure — test that the repository handles it correctly
Collect repository Flow and assert the emitted domain models
Confirm the repository persisted the API response to the local database
Confirm the repository served from cache without hitting the network

sections.heading: Testing Room DAO — In-Memory Database
items: DAO tests require an instrumented test in src/androidTest/ — Room needs the Android framework to compile queries
Use an in-memory Room database: Room.inMemoryDatabaseBuilder(context, AppDatabase::class.java).allowMainThreadQueries().build()
In-memory database is destroyed when the process ends — each test gets a clean state if you create it in @Before
Close the database in @After: database.close() — prevents resource leaks between tests
Test insert and query: dao.insert(fakeUser); val result = dao.getUserById(1); assertThat(result).isEqualTo(fakeUser)
Test Flow queries with Turbine: dao.getAllUsers().test { assertThat(awaitItem()).isEmpty(); dao.insert(fakeUser); assertThat(awaitItem()).containsExactly(fakeUser) }
Test update: dao.insert(fakeUser); dao.update(updatedUser); assertThat(dao.getUserById(1).name).isEqualTo("Updated Name")
Test delete: dao.insert(fakeUser); dao.delete(fakeUser); assertThat(dao.getUserById(1)).isNull()
allowMainThreadQueries() — only for tests — removes the main thread restriction so test assertions can read synchronously

rows.description: A hand-written in-memory implementation of the Repository interface used in tests — simulates real behavior without real data sources
rows.example: class FakeUserRepository : UserRepository { private val users = mutableListOf<User>() }

rows.aspect: When to use Fakes over Mocks
rows.description: Use fakes when the repository has many methods, complex stateful behavior, or when multiple tests share the same fake — fakes are more maintainable than per-test stubbing
rows.example: Fakes for repositories used across ViewModel tests, UseCase tests, and integration tests

rows.description: Add control properties to the fake to simulate different scenarios: var shouldThrowError = false; var delay = 0L
rows.example: if (shouldThrowError) throw IOException(); delay(delay); return users.toList()

rows.description: Use MutableStateFlow or MutableSharedFlow inside the fake to emit updates that the ViewModel observes
Place fake implementations in a dedicated test utility package accessible from both unit and instrumented tests

sections.heading: Testing UseCase Layer
items: UseCases are typically thin classes with a single invoke() operator — they are the simplest layer to test
Mock the repository dependency and test that the UseCase applies the correct business rules
Test happy path: stub repository to return data; invoke useCase(); assert result is correctly transformed or filtered
Test validation: invoke useCase with invalid input; assert it throws IllegalArgumentException or returns an error Result
Test delegation: verify(mockRepository).getUsers() — confirm the UseCase calls the correct repository method
Test data transformation: stub repository to return raw data; assert UseCase maps it to the correct domain model
UseCases that combine multiple repositories: mock all repositories; assert the correct combination logic
Operator function testing: val result = useCase(GetUserUseCase.Params(userId = 1)); assertThat(result).isEqualTo(expectedUser)

rows.viewmodel_test: Stub repo to throw; trigger load; advanceUntilIdle(); assert uiState.value is Error state with correct message
rows.repository_test: Stub API to throw; call repo method in runTest; assert it either rethrows, wraps in Result.failure, or emits error state

rows.viewmodel_test: Stub repo to throw SocketTimeoutException; assert ViewModel emits a timeout-specific error message to UI
rows.repository_test: Stub API to throw SocketTimeoutException; assert repository maps it to a NetworkException or propagates it

rows.viewmodel_test: Stub repo to return empty list; assert ViewModel emits HomeUiState.Empty or equivalent empty state
rows.repository_test: Stub API to return empty body; assert repository returns empty list or emits empty flow — not an error state

rows.viewmodel_test: Stub repo to return Result.failure(exception); assert ViewModel maps failure to correct error UI state
rows.repository_test: Stub API to throw; assert repository catches and wraps in Result.failure with the correct exception type

rows.viewmodel_test: Stub repo to throw then succeed on second call; trigger retry action; assert ViewModel transitions from error to success state
rows.repository_test: Verify API is called the expected number of times; assert repository returns success after retry

sections.heading: Test Data Builders
items: Define default test data builders to avoid repetitive object creation in every test
Use default parameter functions: fun fakeUser(id: Int = 1, name: String = "Alice", email: String = "alice@example.com") = User(id, name, email)
Override only the fields relevant to the current test: val user = fakeUser(name = "Bob") — keeps tests focused
Define fake builders for all domain models: fakeUser(), fakeProduct(), fakeOrder() — place in a shared test utilities file
Use builders for network models too: fakeUserDto(), fakeProductResponse() — to test mapping logic
For complex objects use the Builder pattern: UserBuilder().withId(1).withName("Alice").build()
Place all test data builders in src/test/java/com/example/app/test/data/TestDataBuilders.kt
Consistent test data makes test failures easier to diagnose — a fakeUser() always has known, predictable values

rows.mistake: Not applying MainDispatcherRule in ViewModel tests
rows.fix: Add @get:Rule val mainDispatcherRule = MainDispatcherRule() to every ViewModel test class — without it coroutines launched in viewModelScope fail with dispatcher initialization error
Always mock or fake all repository dependencies — never use real repositories with real databases or network in ViewModel unit tests

rows.mistake: Not advancing coroutines before asserting state
rows.fix: Call advanceUntilIdle() inside runTest { } after triggering async actions — StateFlow does not update until the coroutine completes

rows.mistake: Using real Room database in repository unit tests
rows.fix: Mock the DAO in repository unit tests — use real in-memory Room only in DAO integration tests in src/androidTest/
Write explicit tests for error states, empty states, loading states, and edge cases — these are where real bugs hide

rows.mistake: Creating ViewModel without SavedStateHandle when it is required
rows.fix: Pass SavedStateHandle(mapOf("key" to value)) in the ViewModel constructor call in tests — missing arguments cause NullPointerExceptions in the ViewModel

rows.mistake: Not cancelling Turbine collection at the end of tests
rows.fix: Always call cancelAndIgnoreRemainingEvents() or ensureAllEventsConsumed() at the end of Turbine test blocks to avoid test hanging

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: Testing ViewModel and Repository layers thoroughly is the highest-value investment in your Android test suite. Always apply MainDispatcherRule to every ViewModel test class to replace Dispatchers.Main with a test dispatcher. Use Turbine to assert StateFlow and SharedFlow emissions in sequence — covering loading, success, error, and empty states. Mock all dependencies with Mockito or replace them with hand-written fakes for complex stateful repositories. For the Repository layer, test each caching scenario explicitly — cache hit, cache miss, network error, and stale data. Test Room DAOs using an in-memory database in instrumented tests, verifying query correctness and Flow emissions. Use SavedStateHandle directly in tests to verify argument reading and state restoration. Build shared test data builders for domain models to keep individual tests concise and focused on the specific behavior being verified.
